title: Bridge Paper: Understanding the neural bases of bodily self-consciousness: recent achievements and main challenges.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Physical De-solidification** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Body illusion — multisensory integration, rubber hand illusion, ownership as constructed percept.
**Phase context:** body representation, rubber hand illusion, multisensory integration, body ownership, bodily self

Paper: Understanding the neural bases of bodily self-consciousness: recent achievements and main challenges.
DOI: 10.3389/fnint.2023.1145924
Abstract: The last two decades have seen a surge of interest in the mechanisms underpinning bodily self-consciousness (BSC). Studies showed that BSC relies on several bodily experiences (i.e., self-location, body ownership, agency, first-person perspective) and multisensory integration. The aim of this literature review is to summarize new insights and novel developments into the understanding of the neural bases of BSC, such as the contribution of the interoceptive signals to the neural mechanisms of BSC

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-11. Phase 2 (Physical De-solidification).
